steps.title: Identify the exponential decay model
steps.content: When a quantity declines by a constant percentage each year, it follows exponential decay of the form $C = C_0(b)^t$. Here, $C_0 = 600$ million is the initial value in 2005, and $b$ is the yearly multiplier (what we multiply by each year).

steps.title: Convert the decline rate to a multiplier
steps.content: Declined by 15% each year means we multiply by $1 - 0.15 = 0.85$ each year. So the yearly multiplier is $b = 0.85$. Substituting into the exponential model: $$C = 600(0.85)^t$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
